what_it_does: Defines browser-global React hooks that read from window.CV_REGISTRY, subscribe to registry changes, and expose memoized query/action helpers for types, lineage, children, and slot compatibility.
observations: Wraps all definitions in an IIFE and exits early with a console error if window.CV_REGISTRY is missing.
Each read hook keeps a local tick state and increments it when the registry subscription fires.
Read hooks use useMemo to call registry methods after tick or input changes.
useRegistryActions returns stable wrapper functions for register, update, remove, candidatesForSlot, and accepts.
Exports hooks by assigning them to window properties.
React.useCallback is destructured but not used.
standouts: Registry reactivity is implemented through a shared tick invalidation pattern repeated across hooks.
The file exposes hooks globally rather than through module exports.
useTypes derives its filter dependency with JSON.stringify(filter || null).
conventions: Uses an IIFE for file-local scope around browser globals.
Uses React hooks directly from the global React object.
Names hooks with the use* convention expected by React.
Keeps registry access behind thin hook wrappers.
Assumes R.subscribe can be returned from useEffect as the cleanup function.
concerns: The subscription/tick logic is duplicated across multiple hooks.
JSON.stringify in a dependency array can fail for cyclic filters and may ignore non-JSON filter contents.
The empty dependency arrays rely on the registry global remaining stable after initialization.
notes: This file is a React hook facade over a global type registry, adding subscription-driven refresh behavior for registry reads and stable wrappers for registry actions.
questions: Does R.subscribe return an unsubscribe function suitable for React effect cleanup?
Are filters expected to be JSON-serializable and free of cyclic references?
Is window-level hook export the intended integration surface for all consumers?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it provides reactive React hooks for a universal Type Registry and action helpers that call registry methods.
intention_signals: The file appears intended to make the Type Registry usable from React components without requiring components to manage subscriptions or call registry query methods directly.
novelty: The distinct aspect is the simple tick-based bridge from a browser-global registry subscription into multiple globally exported React hooks.
contribution: Provides a compact React integration layer that a fused system would keep as the registry-facing hook surface for type queries, ancestry reads, child lookups, and registry mutations.
summary_for_embedding: Browser-global React hooks for querying and mutating window.CV_REGISTRY with subscription-triggered re-rendering and memoized registry reads.
intention_for_embedding: This was for letting React components consume a global Type Registry reactively through simple hooks and action wrappers.

symbols.does: Returns registry query results for an optional filter and refreshes when the registry changes.
symbols.description: This hook subscribes to the registry, increments a local tick on updates, and memoizes R.query(filter). Its dependency tracking combines the tick with a JSON string form of the filter.

symbols.does: Returns a single registry type by id and refreshes when the registry changes.
symbols.description: This hook uses the same subscription tick pattern as the other read hooks. It memoizes R.get(id) based on the tick and the requested id.

symbols.does: Returns a resolved type by id using the registry's resolve method.
symbols.description: This hook subscribes to registry changes and recomputes R.resolve(id) when either the registry changes or the id changes. The comment describes the resolved value as a type flattened with its ancestry.

symbols.does: Returns the lineage array for a type id.
symbols.description: This hook subscribes to registry updates and memoizes R.lineage(id). The file comment describes the lineage ordering as self to root.

symbols.does: Returns types that directly extend the given type id.
symbols.description: This hook follows the local subscription tick pattern and memoizes R.children(id). It exposes direct child lookup as a React hook.

symbols.does: Returns stable wrapper functions for registry mutations and slot compatibility checks.
symbols.description: This hook memoizes an object containing register, update, remove, candidates, and accepts functions. Each function delegates directly to the corresponding CV_REGISTRY method.
